仨.origin: 仨 is a friendly way to say three people, joining 亻 and 三.

sentence.en: The three of us are good friends.

仲.origin: 仲 means the middle one, like the middle brother in a family.

sentence.en: It is now midsummer.

价.origin: 价 means price or value, how much something costs.

sentence.en: This price is very cheap.

仿.origin: 仿 means to imitate or copy, like a person doing what another does.

sentence.en: He likes to imitate animals.

企.origin: 企 shows a person 人 on tiptoe 止, hoping or planning ahead.

sentence.en: Penguins live in very cold places.

伊.origin: 伊 is an old word for he or she, now mostly seen in names.

sentence.en: That dear one is by the water.

伍.origin: 伍 pairs a person with five 五, meaning a group or team.

sentence.en: We line up into a team.

伏.origin: 伏 shows a person 亻 lying low like a dog 犬; it means to crouch or hide.

sentence.en: He fell asleep slumped on the desk.

伐.origin: 伐 shows a person and a weapon 戈; it means to cut down or attack.

sentence.en: The workers are cutting down trees.

休.origin: 休 shows a person 亻 resting against a tree 木; it means to rest.

sentence.en: Rest a bit when you are tired.

优.origin: 优 means excellent or superior, better than the rest.

sentence.en: She is an excellent student.

伙.origin: 伙 means a companion or group, people gathered round a fire 火.

sentence.en: He is my good buddy.

伪.origin: 伪 means false or fake, not the real thing.

sentence.en: This is a forged signature.

估.origin: 估 means to guess or estimate the value of something.

sentence.en: I reckon he will come.

伴.origin: 伴 means a partner or companion who goes along with you.

sentence.en: The dog is my good companion.

佃.origin: 佃 shows a person 亻 working a field 田; it means a tenant farmer.

sentence.en: In the past his family were tenant farmers.

住.origin: 住 shows a person 亻 staying in one place; it means to live or stop.

sentence.en: I live in the city.

余.origin: 余 means what is left over, the remainder or surplus.

sentence.en: There is still leftover rice on the plate.

佛.origin: 佛 means Buddha; the person 亻 part shows it is about a being.

sentence.en: There is a Buddha statue in the temple.

作.origin: 作 shows a person 亻 making something; it means to do or make.

sentence.en: Dad went to work.

佟.origin: 佟 is mainly used as a family name.

sentence.en: His surname is Tong.

佩.origin: 佩 means to wear an ornament; it also means to admire someone.

sentence.en: I really admire him.

佰.origin: 佰 is the formal way to write one hundred (百).

sentence.en: The bill says three hundred yuan.

佳.origin: 佳 means good, fine, or beautiful.

sentence.en: Mid-Autumn is a wonderful festival.

侃.origin: 侃 means upright and honest, or to chat freely.

word.en: to talk with ease and confidence

sentence.en: He spoke with ease and confidence.

侉.origin: 侉 means to speak with a heavy accent that sounds different.

sentence.en: His speech has a bit of an accent.

例.origin: 例 means an example or a case used to explain something.

sentence.en: The teacher gave an example.

供.origin: 供 shows a person offering with both hands; it means to supply or provide.

sentence.en: They provide us food and lodging.

依.origin: 依 shows a person 亻 close to clothing 衣; it means to rely on or lean on.

sentence.en: The child leans on its mother.

侧.origin: 侧 means the side, not the front or back.

sentence.en: The door is on the side of the house.

促.origin: 促 shows a person at your feet 足, urging or hurrying you.

sentence.en: Exercise promotes health.

俄.origin: 俄 means sudden or in a moment; it also stands for Russia (俄国).

sentence.en: Russia is big and cold.
This boy is very handsome.

俏.origin: 俏 means pretty and lively, like a smart-looking person.

sentence.en: She looks very pretty.

俗.origin: 俗 means custom or common, the everyday ways of people.

sentence.en: Customs differ from place to place.

俘.origin: 俘 means a prisoner of war who is captured.

sentence.en: He was captured by the enemy.

保.origin: 保 shows a person 亻 holding a child 呆; it means to protect.

sentence.en: We must protect animals.

俯.origin: 俯 means to bow down or look down low.

sentence.en: Look down at the sea from the mountain.

俱.origin: 俱 means all or together, everyone included.

sentence.en: He joined the soccer club.

俺.origin: 俺 is a homely way to say I or me in some dialects.

sentence.en: I am a northerner.

倔.origin: 倔 shows a person who will not bend 屈; it means stubborn.

sentence.en: This ox is very stubborn.

倘.origin: 倘 means if or supposing, used to set up a condition.

sentence.en: If it rains, then don't go.

候.origin: 候 means to wait, or a time or season.

sentence.en: Now it is time to eat.

倚.origin: 倚 means to lean on or rely upon something.

sentence.en: He leans on the wall to rest.

倦.origin: 倦 shows a curled-up person; it means tired or weary.

sentence.en: After walking all day I am very tired.

债.origin: 债 means a debt, money one person owes another.

sentence.en: He paid off his debt.

值.origin: 值 means worth or value, how much something is worth.

sentence.en: This book is very valuable.

倾.origin: 倾 means to lean over or pour out, tipping to one side.

sentence.en: That tree is a bit tilted.

假.origin: 假 means fake or false; with another reading it means holiday.

sentence.en: Do not tell lies.

偈.origin: 偈 names a short Buddhist verse or chant.

sentence.en: The monk recited a Buddhist verse.
